children.text: Summit Home Services has been keeping homes comfortable and running smoothly since 2009. Licensed, bonded, and insured.
Serving the greater metro area including Springfield, Riverside, Oakdale, Fairview, and surrounding communities within 30 miles.

content.headline: Your home deserves the best care
content.subheadline: Licensed plumbing, HVAC, and electrical services for homeowners and businesses. Same-day appointments available. Satisfaction guaranteed.

content.headline: Why homeowners trust us
content.subheadline: Over 15 years of keeping homes comfortable, safe, and running smoothly.

content.headline: Need service today?
content.description: Call now or book online. We offer same-day appointments and 24/7 emergency service.

children.text: Summit Home Services was founded in 2009 by Mike Reeves, a third-generation tradesman who believed homeowners deserved better from their service providers — honest pricing, reliable scheduling, and work you can stand behind.
Our story

What started as a one-truck plumbing operation has grown into a full-service home services company with 12 technicians, a dedicated dispatch team, and a fleet of fully stocked service vehicles. We've completed over 10,000 jobs across the metro area and maintained a 4.9-star rating on Google.
How we work

Every job starts with a thorough inspection and a written estimate. We explain what needs to be done, why, and what it will cost — before we pick up a tool. We clean up after ourselves, and we don't consider the job done until you're satisfied. It's not complicated, but you'd be surprised how many companies skip these basics.
Our commitment

We're licensed, bonded, and insured in the state. Every technician passes a background check and completes ongoing training. We carry $2M in liability coverage because your home matters to us as much as it does to you.

content.headline: Get in touch
content.subheadline: Request a quote, schedule service, or ask a question. We respond within 1 business hour during working hours.

data.title: 5 Signs It's Time to Replace Your Water Heater
data.excerpt: Most water heaters last 8-12 years. Here's how to tell if yours is on its way out — before it floods your basement.

$media.alt: Plumber working on pipes under a sink

children.text: Your water heater works hard every day — showers, dishes, laundry. Most homeowners don't think about it until something goes wrong. Here are five warning signs that your water heater is telling you it's time for a replacement.
1. It's over 10 years old

Tank water heaters typically last 8-12 years. Check the serial number on yours — the first two digits are usually the year it was manufactured. If it's approaching or past 10, start planning for a replacement before it fails unexpectedly.
2. Rusty or discolored water
If rusty water only comes from the hot side, your tank is likely corroding from the inside. This can't be repaired. Once corrosion starts, a leak isn't far behind.
3. Strange noises

Popping, cracking, or rumbling sounds mean sediment has hardened at the bottom of the tank. This makes the heater work harder, increases your energy bill, and accelerates wear. Flushing the tank annually can prevent this, but once the noise starts, the damage is often done.
4. Water pooling around the base
Any moisture around your water heater is a red flag. Small leaks become big leaks fast. If you see water, call a plumber the same day — a 50-gallon tank failure can cause serious water damage.
5. You're running out of hot water faster
If your morning showers are getting shorter because the hot water runs out, sediment buildup is reducing your tank's effective capacity. At this point, you're paying to heat water you can't even use.
If you're seeing any of these signs, give us a call. We'll inspect your current unit and walk you through your options — traditional tank, tankless, or hybrid. Free estimates, no pressure.

data.title: 6 Ways to Prepare Your AC for Summer (Before It's 95 Degrees)
data.excerpt: Don't wait until the first heat wave to find out your AC isn't working. A little spring maintenance goes a long way.

$media.alt: Outdoor air conditioning units beside a house

children.text: Every year we get a flood of emergency calls on the first really hot day of summer. Most of those breakdowns could have been prevented with 30 minutes of spring maintenance. Here's what you can do right now to avoid being stuck in a 90-degree house.
1. Change your air filter

This is the simplest and most impactful thing you can do. A dirty filter restricts airflow, makes your system work harder, and can freeze your evaporator coil. Change it every 1-3 months, or monthly if you have pets.
2. Clear debris from your outdoor unit
Your condenser needs good airflow to work properly. Clear away leaves, grass clippings, and any vegetation within 2 feet of the unit. Gently hose off the fins to remove dust and pollen buildup.
3. Test it before you need it

Turn your AC on now, while it's still cool out. Set it 5 degrees below room temperature and let it run for 15 minutes. Check that cold air is coming from the vents and the outdoor unit is running. Better to discover a problem now than on a 100-degree Saturday.
Want a professional tune-up? Our spring AC checkup is $89 and includes a 21-point inspection, refrigerant check, and coil cleaning. Schedule yours before the rush.

data.title: 7 Simple Ways to Lower Your Energy Bill This Month
data.excerpt: You don't need to spend thousands on upgrades. These practical tips can cut your energy bill by 10-25% starting today.

$media.alt: Smart thermostat on a wall showing temperature

children.text: Heating and cooling account for almost half of the average home's energy costs. The good news is that some of the most effective ways to save money don't require any tools or a service call.
1. Set your thermostat and leave it

Every degree you raise your thermostat in summer (or lower in winter) saves about 3% on your bill. Set it to 78 in summer and 68 in winter. A programmable or smart thermostat makes this automatic — set it back when you're sleeping or away.
2. Seal air leaks around windows and doors

A $5 tube of caulk and $10 worth of weatherstripping can save you hundreds per year. Check around windows, doors, and where pipes enter your home. If you can feel a draft, you're paying to heat or cool the outside.
Want a professional energy audit? We offer a full home energy assessment that identifies exactly where you're losing money. The assessment is free when combined with any service call.

data.title: Plumbing Services
data.summary: From leaky faucets to full repiping. Licensed plumbers available same-day for repairs, installations, and emergencies.

$media.alt: Plumber working under a kitchen sink

children.text: Whether it's a dripping faucet, a clogged drain, or a complete bathroom remodel, our licensed plumbers handle it all. We stock our trucks with parts for the most common repairs so we can often fix the problem in a single visit.
What we do

Drain cleaning and unclogging, faucet and fixture repair/installation, water heater repair and replacement (tank and tankless), toilet repair and installation, garbage disposal installation, pipe repair and repiping, sump pump installation, water line repair, gas line repair, and sewer line inspection and repair.

data.title: Heating & Air Conditioning
data.summary: Keep your home comfortable year-round. AC and furnace repair, installation, and seasonal maintenance.

$media.alt: HVAC technician inspecting an air conditioning unit

children.text: A well-maintained HVAC system keeps your family comfortable and your energy bills under control. We service all major brands and offer seasonal maintenance plans that catch small problems before they become expensive emergencies.
What we do

AC repair and installation, furnace repair and installation, heat pump systems, ductwork repair and sealing, thermostat installation (including smart thermostats), indoor air quality solutions, seasonal tune-ups, and emergency 24/7 repair.

data.title: Electrical Services
data.summary: Safe, code-compliant electrical work. Panel upgrades, rewiring, lighting, outlets, and EV charger installation.

$media.alt: Electrician working on a circuit breaker panel

children.text: Electrical work isn't something to DIY. Our licensed electricians handle everything from adding an outlet to upgrading your entire panel — safely, to code, and with a warranty on all work.
What we do

Electrical panel upgrades, whole-home rewiring, outlet and switch installation, lighting installation (recessed, under-cabinet, landscape), ceiling fan installation, EV charger installation, generator installation, surge protection, code violation correction, and safety inspections.

data.title: 24/7 Emergency Service
data.summary: Burst pipe at 2 AM? Furnace out in January? We're here 24/7 for plumbing, HVAC, and electrical emergencies.

children.text: Emergencies don't wait for business hours, and neither do we. Our on-call technicians are available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year. We aim to be at your door within 60 minutes for true emergencies.
What counts as an emergency?

Burst or frozen pipes, sewer backups, no heat in winter, gas leaks (call 911 first, then us), flooding from appliance failure, complete loss of power to part of your home, and any situation that poses an immediate safety risk.

data.name: Mike Reeves
data.bio: Third-generation tradesman with 25+ years in the field. Founded Summit in 2009 because he believed homeowners deserved honest pricing and reliable service. Still does at least one service call a week because he loves the work.

data.name: Diana Chen
data.bio: Keeps the schedule running, the trucks stocked, and the team on track. If you call the office, Diana's probably the friendly voice on the other end. 8 years with Summit.

data.name: Marcus Johnson
data.bio: EPA-certified with 12 years of experience in residential and commercial HVAC. Specializes in high-efficiency system installations and can diagnose a compressor problem by sound alone.

data.name: Jenny Alvarez
data.bio: Journeyman electrician with a focus on panel upgrades and whole-home rewiring. Known for keeping the cleanest work sites on the team. 6 years with Summit.

data.author: Karen M.
data.quote: Our AC died on the hottest day of the year. Summit had a tech here within 2 hours and had it running by dinner. They even called the next day to make sure everything was still working. Lifesavers.

data.author: Robert T.
data.quote: Finally found a plumber I can trust. Fair prices, shows up on time, cleans up after the job. They replaced our water heater and the whole thing took 3 hours. I won't call anyone else.

data.author: Linda & Steve P.
data.quote: They rewired our entire 1960s kitchen as part of a renovation. Professional crew, clean work, passed inspection on the first try. They walked us through every step and even helped us understand our panel capacity for future projects.

data.author: David H.
data.quote: We use Summit for all 12 of our rental properties. They're responsive, reasonably priced, and the tenants always have good things to say about the technicians. That matters a lot in property management.

data.author: Maria G.
data.quote: Our furnace quit on Christmas Eve. Mike himself came out at 9 PM and had it running by 10:30. Didn't even charge the holiday rate. You don't find companies like this anymore.

data.title: Elmwood Kitchen Remodel
data.summary: Complete plumbing rough-in and finish work for a full kitchen renovation — relocated water lines, new gas line for range, and garbage disposal install.

$media.alt: Modern kitchen with white cabinets and farmhouse sink

children.text: The Pattersons were gutting their 1970s kitchen and needed all plumbing relocated to accommodate a new island with a prep sink. We worked closely with their general contractor to rough in new supply and drain lines, install a gas line for their range, and finish with a farmhouse sink and commercial-style faucet.
Scope

Relocated main sink supply and drain. Ran new copper supply lines to island. Installed gas line for 6-burner range. Set farmhouse sink and commercial pull-down faucet. Installed garbage disposal and dishwasher connection. All work passed inspection on the first visit.

data.title: Riverside Dental Office HVAC
data.summary: Designed and installed a zoned HVAC system for a 3,200 sq ft dental office — 4 zones for exam rooms, lobby, and back office.

children.text: Dr. Rivera was opening a new dental practice and needed a system that could keep exam rooms cool (dental equipment generates a lot of heat) while maintaining a comfortable temperature in the waiting area. We designed a 4-zone system with independent thermostats so each area is dialed in.
Result

The high-efficiency heat pump system with variable speed blower keeps operating costs low while giving each zone precise temperature control. Energy costs came in 30% below Dr. Rivera's budget projections for the first year.

data.title: 1950s Whole-Home Rewire
data.summary: Complete electrical rewire of a 2,100 sq ft 1950s ranch — replaced knob-and-tube wiring, upgraded to 200A panel, and added 40 new circuits.

$media.alt: Electrical panel with neatly organized wiring

children.text: The Chens bought a beautiful mid-century ranch but the electrical hadn't been touched since it was built. The original knob-and-tube wiring was a fire hazard, and the 60-amp panel couldn't support modern appliances. We did a full rewire without tearing out walls, upgraded to a 200A panel, and added circuits for a home office, EV charger, and workshop.

data.question: How much does a service call cost?

children.text: Our standard service call fee is $89 for plumbing and HVAC, $99 for electrical. This covers the trip to your home and a full diagnostic. If you proceed with the repair, the service call fee is applied toward the total cost of the job. After-hours and emergency calls are $149.

data.question: How fast can you get here for an emergency?

children.text: For true emergencies (burst pipes, no heat in winter, gas leaks, flooding), we aim to have a technician at your door within 60 minutes. For urgent but non-emergency issues, we typically offer same-day service if you call before noon.

We serve Springfield and the surrounding communities within 30 miles, including Riverside, Oakdale, Fairview, Cedar Hills, and Lakeview. If you're not sure whether you're in our service area, give us a call and we'll let you know.

data.question: What payment methods do you accept?

children.text: We accept cash, check, and all major credit cards. For larger projects ($1,000+), we also offer financing through GreenSky with approved credit — 0% interest for 12 months on qualifying purchases. Payment is due upon completion of work.

Yes — for larger projects like system replacements, repiping, panel upgrades, or remodeling work, we provide free written estimates. For repair work, the $89-99 service call covers the diagnostic and you'll receive an upfront quote before any work begins.

Yes. Summit Home Services is fully licensed, bonded, and insured. We carry $2 million in general liability coverage. All plumbers, HVAC technicians, and electricians hold active state licenses and are background-checked. We're happy to provide license numbers and proof of insurance upon request.

data.question: How do I schedule an appointment?

children.text: You can call us at (555) 234-5678, fill out the contact form on our website, or send us a text at the same number. Our office is staffed Monday through Friday 7 AM to 6 PM and Saturday 8 AM to 2 PM. For after-hours emergencies, our answering service will dispatch a technician.

Every repair comes with a minimum 90-day warranty on labor. New equipment installations carry the manufacturer's warranty (typically 5-10 years on parts) plus our own 2-year labor warranty. If something we installed or repaired fails within the warranty period, we'll make it right at no charge.
